raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A general opinion request on a research-section excerpt centrally includes checking whether the stated methods, measurements, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The artifact makes convergence and robustness claims tied to reported tables and experiments, so judging whether those claims follow from the reported evidence is central.","R1.5":"The excerpt is a section-level research narrative, so feedback would reasonably address ordering, redundancy, and narrative arc across the section.","R1.6":"The broad request strongly invites sentence- and paragraph-level assessment, especially because the artifact contains conspicuous repetition and phrasing issues.","RX.1":"Any projection of a vague opinion request must preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and criteria of evaluation.","RX.2":"The responsibility includes avoiding stronger judgments than the artifact supports, especially where tables or external evidence are not provided.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not actually critique or rewrite the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid encouraging unsupported research claims or misleading conclusions downstream.","RX.5":"The projection should keep responsibility assignments traceable to the delegation wording and the specific features of the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.2: A general opinion request on a research-section excerpt centrally includes checking whether the stated methods, measurements, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes convergence and robustness claims tied to reported tables and experiments, so judging whether those claims follow from the reported evidence is central.
rationale.R1.5: The excerpt is a section-level research narrative, so feedback would reasonably address ordering, redundancy, and narrative arc across the section.
rationale.R1.6: The broad request strongly invites sentence- and paragraph-level assessment, especially because the artifact contains conspicuous repetition and phrasing issues.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague opinion request must preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and criteria of evaluation.
rationale.RX.2: The responsibility includes avoiding stronger judgments than the artifact supports, especially where tables or external evidence are not provided.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not actually critique or rewrite the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid encouraging unsupported research claims or misleading conclusions downstream.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should keep responsibility assignments traceable to the delegation wording and the specific features of the artifact.
